lyrics:
Don’t it make you sad to know that life
is more than who we are

hints.content: This song was released in September 1995.
The band’s biggest hit was part of the soundtrack for City of Angels.

Here are some other lyrics from the song:

You grew up way too fast
And now there’s nothing to believe
Then reruns all become our history
A tired song keeps playing on a tired radio
And I won’t tell no one your name
/audio/Name Hint.wav

explainer.heading: Song: Name — Artist: The Goo Goo Dolls — Album: A Boy Named Goo
explainer.text: This line always struck home because of the way it acknowledges that the things that are important to us in our own little lives probably seem petty and insignificant if we just draw back the lens and think beyond ourselves — but it doesn’t make the point in a moralizing way. It’s not saying, “Stop caring about the things that don’t matter,” it’s saying, hey, the fact that the things you care about don’t matter to the wider world — that really sucks, doesn’t it? And that’s so validating to hear.

lyrics: My dreams are bursting at the seams

hints.content: This song was inspired by the singer’s insomnia.

Here are some other lyrics from the song:

A foxtrot above my head
A sock hop beneath my bed
A disco ball is just hanging by a thread
The artist was asked to produce a pop version of “Waving through the Window” by the writers of “Dear Evan Hansen.”
/images/Fireflies Robot.png

explainer.heading: Song: Fireflies — Artist: Owl City — Album: Ocean Eyes
explainer.text: It’s basically a signature for Owl City (a.k.a. Adam Young) to write songs that are optimistic, unjaded, and full of imagination and childlike wonder. This line feels like a peek into the mind behind that signature — a mind flooded with so many hopes and ideas and excitement about the possibilities of the future that it feels like it could burst. To me, it lands as a reminder to tamp down the cynicism, and aspire for some of that wonder and excitement about the open future.

lyrics:
Oh, where do we begin?
The rubble, or our sins?

hints.content: Another of the band’s singles featured Marshmello.
This song was released in 2013 and is named after an ancient city.

Here are some other lyrics from the song:

And if you close your eyes
Does it almost feel like you’ve been here before?
How am I gonna be an optimist about this?
/audio/Pompeii Hint.wav

explainer.heading: Song: Pompeii — Artist: Bastille — Album: Bad Blood
explainer.text: Tragedy is a part of life. It happens to everyone at some point, even if not usually on the scale of Pompeii getting obliterated by a volcano. And sometimes the tragedy just happens, but sometimes it feels like we bring it on ourselves, because of our bad decisions, karma, or whatever it is. And when you’re picking up the pieces after that kind of tragedy, boy does this question pack a punch. Do I start with the logistics of my life that have to change? Or do I start with my attitude, my selfishness, my fear of commitment, or whatever landed me there? Where do we begin?

lyrics:
I can’t remember all the times I tried to tell myself
to hold on to these moments as they pass

hints.content: The music video for this song features Courteney Cox.
Some of the band’s other singles include “Round Here” and “Mr. Jones.”

Here are some other lyrics from the song:

And it’s one more day up in the canyons
And it’s one more night in Hollywood
If you think that I could be forgiven
I wish you would
/audio/Long December Hint.wav

explainer.heading: Song: A Long December — Artist: Counting Crows — Album: Recovering the Satellites
explainer.text: This line captures a crazy thing about being human: just because you were there doesn’t mean you experienced it. Even in the very best moments, if you’re not present, if you’re consumed with something else, dwelling on the past or worrying about the future, then you miss it. But even if you know it, that doesn’t make it easy. You can try and try to be present and fail, and feel like the best things are passing you by but you can’t get out of your head long enough to feel them. We just gotta keep trying. (And meditate!)

lyrics:
We got the bubble-headed bleach-blonde who comes on at five
She can tell you about the plane crash with a gleam in her eye
It’s interesting when people die

hints.content: The singer was also part of the band that sang Hotel California.

Kick ’em when they’re up

Kick ’em when they’re up

/audio/Dirty Laundry Hint.wav

explainer.heading: Song: Dirty Laundry — Artist: Don Henley — Album: I Can’t Stand Still
explainer.text: This song, a cutting indictment of the news media, feels like it could’ve been written yesterday as easily as 1982. Henley calls out the industry for the fact that it has a built-in incentive to report tragic, scandalous or otherwise sensational news, and thus warps the ways news personnel experience all those things. The song implicitly calls for us to figure out a way for the news media to be able to inform while also being able to stay human.

lyrics:
So when weakness turns my ego up
I know you’ll count on the me from yesterday

hints.content: Some of the artist’s albums include Make Yourself and Morning View.
The lead singer is also skilled at painting, and has published collections of his artwork.

Here are some other lyrics from the song:

Sing this song
Remind me that we’ll always have each other
When everything else is gone
/images/Dig Image.jpeg

explainer.heading: Song: Dig — Artist: Incubus — Album: Light Grenades
explainer.text: We’re never the same person all the time with everyone in every situation. And sometimes who we are at a given point in time can be a little ugly. But if we’re lucky, we have people in our lives that can see through who we happen to be at our low points, to who we were before, or who we are at our best. And if we’re really lucky, they can help us find our way back or our way out. This song celebrates having that kind of relationship.

lyrics:
Hope that you spend your days
but they all add up

hints.content: The artist’s first hit single was mixed and released by Timbaland.
Some of the artist’s albums include Dreaming Out Loud, Oh My My and Waking Up.

Here are some other lyrics from the song:

I owned every second that this world could give
I saw so many places, the things that I did
Yeah, with every broken bone
I swear I lived
/images/I Lived Image.jpeg

explainer.heading: Song: I Lived — Artist: OneRepublic — Album: Native
explainer.text: This clever play on words, paradoxically wishing for us to have more after we spend everything, is a nod to two pitfalls in life: one, letting our time go by instead of spending it — using it, choosing how we use it — and two, regretting our experiences because they don’t fit the narrative we want. Here’s to hoping we grab life by the horns and love the ride for what it is.

lyrics:
Oh yeah, life goes on
long after the thrill of living is gone

hints.content: Some of the artist’s other songs include Pink Houses and Hurts So Good.
The artist cofounded Farm Aid, which has raised over $60 million for farm families.

Here are some other lyrics from the song:

Holdin’ on to sixteen as long as you can
Change is coming ’round real soon
Make us women and men
/images/Jack and Diane Image.png

explainer.heading: Song: Jack and Diane — Artist: John Mellencamp — Album: American Fool
explainer.text: I must have heard this song fifty times before I really paid attention to these words — the wistful, folksy tone doesn’t lead you to expect lyrics that are so heavy. The song is about two high school sweethearts growing up, and the fun and optimism and ambition of being a teenager that disappears as you get older. We might all try “holding on to sixteen as long as you can,” but at the end of the day, life gets less shiny, the colors get a little more dull, but it still goes on. (Which makes it wonderfully ironic that Jessica Simpson sampled this track for a bubblegum pop love song.)

lyrics:
She said like it or not it’s the way it’s gotta be
You’ve gotta love yourself if you can ever love me

hints.content: The artist’s first album was No Name Face.
Several of the artist’s songs were featured on the show Smallville.

Here are some other lyrics from the song:

I know what’s at stake
I know that I’ve let you down
And if you give me a chance
Believe that I can change
I’ll keep us together
/audio/Whatever It Takes Hint.wav

explainer.heading: Song: Whatever It Takes — Artist: Lifehouse — Album: Who We Are
explainer.text: A huge part of being a good friend or partner is listening, supporting, and bringing good vibes. But that’s one sided, even if it is the “nicer” side. In the deepest relationships, both sides have to be generous enough to be there when needed, and vulnerable enough to need. And for a lot of us that’s really hard, because having needs means looking inside, knowing where we’re incomplete, and letting someone else see that. That’s what these lyrics are about — one partner telling the other: accept yourself enough to trust me to see inside and still love you. That’s how you can love me. Just saying, that girl’s a catch.

lyrics: You don't know me now </br> I kinda thought that you should somehow

hints.content: The singer of this song collaborated with Santana, making a track that won three Grammys and Billboard lists as the third most-successful song ever.
The artist's debut album was Yourself or Someone Like You.

Here are some other lyrics from the song: </br></br>Do you think you can cope? </br> You figured me out </br>That I'm lost and I'm hopeless </br>I'm bleeding and broken </br> Though I've never spoken </br> I come undone
audio/Mad Season Hint.wav

explainer.heading: Song: Mad Season | Artist: Matchbox 20 | Album: Mad Season
explainer.text: Oof. It’s classic Matchbox 20 to sing about a dysfunctional relationship, and this line stands out to me because of the casual, nonchalant yet subtly seething tone of the singer’s observation that hey, I thought this relationship was really deep, I thought this whole time we were together you noticed me, learned about me, cared about who I was. I relied on that, trusted that. Um, guess not? Part of what I love about these lyrics is the way they’re not dramatic. There’s something almost funny about them. And there’s something dignified about them too, how he’s not falling to pieces, this horrible realization hits him yet he’s passive-aggressively playing the detached observer musing about it. So much personality in this, wow.
